prompt:
You are a PROOF-FILLER. Replace the `sorry` placeholders for the listed declarations with complete, type-correct Lean proofs. Do NOT change statement signatures; do NOT weaken hypotheses.

## Integrity (HARD RULES — non-negotiable)
- Close a `sorry` ONLY with a genuine proof. NEVER discharge it with a device that makes the file compile without actually proving the statement: no new `axiom`, no `admit`, no unproven auxiliary `axiom`/lemma cited by `exact`, no `native_decide`/`decide` on a claim that is not genuinely decidable, no `@[implemented_by]`/`@[extern]` or `sorry` hidden inside a helper. Introducing an axiom for the hard part is a FAILURE, not a pass — it silently injects an assumption into the library.
- If a declaration is genuinely beyond you this turn, LEAVE its `sorry` exactly as it is (do not replace it with a fake discharge) and report honestly that you could not prove it, naming the decl and the concrete blocker (missing lemma, unclear step, integrability side-condition, etc.). A `sorry` left in place and reported is the CORRECT outcome when you don't know the proof; a fake discharge is never acceptable.

Lean files live under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling
Target declarations: hoeffding_cov_identity_prod

## Instructions
File: CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/Hoeffding.lean (Lean 4 + Mathlib, repo root /<repo-root>/CausalSmith).

Fill the single `sorry`: `hoeffding_cov_identity_prod`. EDIT ONLY THIS FILE. Do not change statements/hypotheses, no axioms, no new `sorry`. Verify: `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.Optimality` (expect only `sorry` warnings coming from the upstream files `TailIntegral`/`Survival`/`HoeffdingFubini`, which are being proven in parallel — treat their lemmas as available).

Goal:
```
(∫ p, p.1 * p.2 ∂π) - (∫ x, x ∂μ) * (∫ y, y ∂ν)
  = ∫ q : ℝ × ℝ, (jointCdf π q.1 q.2 - cdf μ q.1 * cdf ν q.2) ∂(volume.prod volume)
```
Available (see `HoeffdingFubini.lean`, `Survival.lean`, and `fiber_sub_mean_prod` proven just above in this file):
- `integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber h hμ hν : (∫ p, p.1*p.2 ∂π) = ∫ q, (∫ p, signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2 ∂π) ∂(volume.prod volume)`
- `mean_fst_tail h hμ : (∫ x, x ∂μ) = ∫ s, (survFst π s - tailInd 0 s) ∂volume`
- `mean_snd_tail h hν : (∫ y, y ∂ν) = ∫ t, (survSnd π t - tailInd 0 t) ∂volume`
- `integrable_fiber h hμ hν` (PROVEN), `integrable_survFst_sub h hμ`, `integrable_survSnd_sub h hν`
- `fiber_sub_mean_prod h q : (∫ p, signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2 ∂π) - (survFst π q.1 - tailInd 0 q.1) * (survSnd π q.2 - tailInd 0 q.2) = jointCdf π q.1 q.2 - cdf μ q.1 * cdf ν q.2` (PROVEN)
- Mathlib: `MeasureTheory.integral_prod_mul (f) (g) : ∫ z, f z.1 * g z.2 ∂(μ.prod ν) = (∫ f) * (∫ g)`, `MeasureTheory.integral_sub`, `MeasureTheory.Integrable.mul_prod`.

Sketch:
1. `rw [integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber h hμ hν, mean_fst_tail h hμ, mean_snd_tail h hν]`.
2. Turn the product of the two one-dimensional integrals back into a double integral: `rw [← integral_prod_mul (fun s => survFst π s - tailInd 0 s) (fun t => survSnd π t - tailInd 0 t)]`.
3. `rw [← integral_sub (integrable_fiber h hμ hν) ((integrable_survFst_sub h hμ).mul_prod (integrable_survSnd_sub h hν))]`.
4. `exact integral_congr_ae (Filter.Eventually.of_forall (fiber_sub_mean_prod h))` — or `refine integral_congr_ae ?_; filter_upwards with q using fiber_sub_mean_prod h q`.

If `integral_prod_mul` needs `RCLike L`, `ℝ` qualifies. `SFinite` on `volume` is automatic. You may need `letI : IsProbabilityMeasure π := h.isProbabilityMeasure`. Keep the proof short; if a `rw` fails on the exact shape, use `calc` with `show` to match the lambda forms.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you genuinely closed, which you left as `sorry` (with the concrete blocker for each), and — required — confirm you introduced NO `axiom`/`admit`/other non-proof discharge. No JSON required.
